accuracyDisclaimer: City Planning provides massing data for information and illustrative purposes only; no warranty of fitness for any precision purpose.

Lidar-Derived.citation: Sigma anchored on observed building-height error, not bare-ground LiDAR point accuracy. 88 Scott (Lidar-Derived) reads 197.7 m vs CTBUH 198.2 m, a 0.5 m discrepancy. NRCan CQL1 specifies bare-ground RMSEz <= 0.10 m, but rooftop returns on tall/irregular surfaces degrade to ~0.2-0.3 m and AVG_HEIGHT is a footprint-averaged value. 0.5 m is the observed building-height discrepancy, used as a defensible floor. https://natural-resources.canada.ca/science-data/science-research/natural-hazards/flood-mapping/federal-airborne-lidar-data-acquisition-guideline
Lidar-Derived._note_uncertainty: Based on n=1 observed building-height discrepancy plus the national LiDAR floor. A 0.10 m sigma (bare-ground point accuracy) implies sub-decimetre shadow precision on a 200 m tower, which is false precision and violates the refuse-false-precision rule. 0.5 m is conservative given the small sample; raise it if more discrepancies are observed.

Site Plan.note: Site-plan heights are design or permit-submission figures and may include mechanical penthouses, parapets, or rounded-up floor heights not present in the as-built structure. Observed cases: (1) One York Street reads AVG_HEIGHT=160.4 m in the dataset vs. as-built roof of 156.8 m per CTBUH Skyscraper Center (https://www.skyscrapercenter.com/building/one-york-street/15431), an overshoot of +3.6 m (~2.3%). (2) In a prior cross-check session, the same building was found at +24 m overshoot in a different polygon cluster (a podium-fragmentation artifact, not a true site-plan error). The consistent, non-fragmented overshoot is ~3–5 m. sigma_m=5.0 m is set conservatively to bound typical mechanical-penthouse inclusion (~1 storey ≈ 3 m) with margin.

defaultHeightAccuracy.note: Conservative sigma for features where HEIGHT_SRC is null, '3D Model', 'Photogrammetrics', 'Oblique Aerials', or any unlisted source. Set equal to the Site Plan sigma (the less accurate of the two documented categories) because unknown-source features may have been derived from design drawings, photogrammetric workflows of varying vintage, or manual digitising. Does not apply to Lidar-Derived features, which use sigma_m=0.10 m.

bandScopeNote: v1 band is height-only; footprint geometry error, horizontal position error, and building fragmentation (podium vs. tower polygon splitting) are excluded from the uncertainty band and are listed in the do-NOT-measure list.

_sigmaDerivationNotes.LiDAR_basis: Anchored on observed building-height error (88 Scott: 0.5 m), not the 0.10 m bare-ground RMSEz. Rooftop returns degrade to 0.2-0.3 m; AVG_HEIGHT is footprint-averaged. sigma_m=0.5 m as a defensible floor; n=1, raise with more observations.
_sigmaDerivationNotes.SitePlan_basis: Observed One York overshoot: +3.6 m. One-storey penthouse margin adds ~3 m. sigma_m=5.0 m chosen as 1-sigma bound covering ~95% of expected overestimates within 10 m (2-sigma). Consistent with typical construction tolerance literature for high-rise permit drawings.
_sigmaDerivationNotes.default_basis: Set to Site Plan sigma because no more specific provenance is available. Conservative choice per project honesty principle.
